method: aggregator sweep (myscheme/india.gov.in/vikaspedia dead-ends; drishtiias-class explainers + subsidy-consultant sites as SECONDARY; MeitY PLI FAQ + PMFME-AIF SOP + PIB as OFFICIAL anchors)
note: No aggregator -- official or private -- maintains stacking info systematically; this layer has no existing substitute.

interlinkages.detail: MeitY PLI FAQ (Clause 3.6): 'Eligibility under PLI Scheme shall not affect eligibility under any other scheme and vice-versa. A company may apply under both the schemes... incentive disbursal under PLI and SPECS scheme will be independent of each other and also not linked to eligibility and disbursement of incentives under any other central or state government schemes.' Same capex can count under both PLI and SPECS if each scheme's own criteria are met. This is the explicit central-side legal basis for the PLI + state top-up stack.
interlinkages.source: meity.gov.in/static/uploads/2024/02/Frequently_Asked_Questions_on_PLI_Scheme.pdf (fetched, pdftotext extracted)

schemes: State capital subsidy + SGST reimbursement policies
interlinkages.detail: States deliberately design top-ups on central schemes: Gujarat's Aatmanirbhar scheme treats any central-PLI-listed sector as an automatic 'sunrise sector' eligible for higher state incentives on top of central PLI; UP Electronics Component Manufacturing Policy 2025 offers explicit 'ECMS top-up' capital subsidy; Maharashtra PSI 2019 SGST refund is claimed alongside PLI sales incentives (consultant-documented example: Rs 3.2 cr combined Year-1 benefit for one electronics client). Typical state SGST reimbursement is 30-100% of cash-paid SGST capped to fixed capital investment over 5-10 years.
interlinkages.source: finraja.com (UP ECMS top-up), entrepreneurindia.co (Gujarat), rmpsco.com + bizastra.com (SGST/PSI stacking practice)

interlinkages.detail: Formal convergence module launched Sep 2022 (PIB PRID 1861268) with a published SOP: a PMFME beneficiary with credit-linked 35% subsidy can additionally apply on the AIF portal using the already-approved DPR and sanction letter to get AIF's 3% interest subvention on the same project loan; conversely AIF beneficiaries eligible under PMFME can apply on the PMFME MIS portal with the AIF sanction letter for the capital subsidy. Bank ROI norms for the converged loan are published (pmfmeap.org 'Rate of Interest_Loans under PMFME_convergence with AIF').
interlinkages.source: pmfme.mofpi.gov.in convergence SOP + instruction PDFs; pib.gov.in PRID 1861268

interlinkages.detail: Same convergence module and SOP covers PMKSY of MoFPI: PMKSY credit-linked subsidy beneficiaries can layer AIF 3% interest subvention (AIF terms: Rs 1 lakh crore fund, 3% subvention on loans up to Rs 2 crore, max 7 years).
interlinkages.source: pmfme.mofpi.gov.in SOP 'Convergence of AIF of DA&FW with PMFME and PMKSY of MoFPI'; agri.bot/aif

interlinkages.detail: AHIDF (Rs 29,610 cr, 3% interest subvention, up to 90% bank loan for dairy/meat/feed/breeding infra) deliberately excludes pure fisheries projects to avoid double-funding with PMMSY; fisheries infra goes to PMMSY. National Livestock Mission's entrepreneurship sub-component (capital subsidy on smaller sheep/goat/poultry/piggery/feed projects) is positioned as complementary to AHIDF's medium-ticket processing focus, not overlapping.
interlinkages.source: agri.bot/ahidf (explainer aggregating DAHD guidelines)

schemes: PM Surya Ghar Muft Bijli Yojana
interlinkages.detail: Clean segmentation: PM Surya Ghar (launched 15 Feb 2024) = residential rooftop only — up to Rs 78,000 subsidy, 300 free units/month, 1 crore households. PM-KUSUM = agricultural only — Component A (500 kW-2 MW decentralized plants on barren/stilted farmland), Component B (standalone solar pumps), Component C (solarisation of grid-connected pumps + feeder-level solarisation, surplus sold to DISCOM). No stacking case exists because beneficiary classes and asset classes are disjoint. All KUSUM components currently valid till 31 Dec 2026.
interlinkages.source: MNRE PM Surya Ghar guidelines PDF (cdnbbsr.s3waas.gov.in); pmsuryaghar.gov.in; alpexsolar/vikramsolar explainers

interlinkages.detail: Gati Shakti NMP is the meta-coordination layer, not a funding scheme: it 'will incorporate the infrastructure schemes of various Ministries and State Governments like Bharatmala, Sagarmala, inland waterways, dry/land ports, UDAN'. Started with 16 ministries (Oct 2021); now 44 central ministries + 36 states/UTs onboarded with 1,614 geospatial data layers; every department gets visibility of others' project pipelines for synchronized planning. Individual scheme outlays remain with their nodal ministries.
interlinkages.source: pib.gov.in PRID 1763638; pmgatishakti.gov.in/about; negd.gov.in NMP case study

interlinkages.detail: PMDDKY (2025-26 to 2030-31, 100 districts) has NO separate budget line — it converges the budgets of 36 existing schemes from 11 departments (incl. PM-KISAN, PMFBY, PMKSY-irrigation) at Rs 24,000 crore/year (Rs 1.44 lakh cr total), executed through District Agriculture Development Plans under Collector-chaired DDKY Samitis, monitored on 117 KPIs monthly. It is the agriculture-sector analogue of Gati Shakti-style convergence: an allocation-routing layer, not a new incentive.
interlinkages.source: pmindia.gov.in cabinet approval; drishtiias.com/daily-updates/daily-news-analysis/pm-dhan-dhaanya-krishi-yojana-1

interlinkages.detail: DFPD interest subvention schemes (six notifications 2018-2022; 6% p.a. or 50% of bank rate, whichever lower, for 5 years incl. 1-year moratorium) financed distillery capacity explicitly to meet EBP blending targets and relieve sugar-mill liquidity from surplus sugar; extended to grain-based ethanol in 2021. A 2025 scheme funds cooperative sugar mills converting cane-only ethanol plants to multi-feedstock (maize, damaged foodgrains) — de-risking EBP supply from cane cycles (PIB PRID 2109157).
interlinkages.source: nabard.org scheme page; pib.gov.in PRID 2109157; nsws.gov.in Ethanol Policy page

interlinkages.detail: Feedstock-sharing overlap rather than exclusion: SATAT CBG plants are frequently co-located with ethanol distilleries and sugar mills (press-mud, spent wash, bagasse co-gen), forming integrated bio-refineries; explainers cite 8-12% plant-economics improvement from co-location. Same agri-residue/press-mud feedstock pool serves both programmes — a real allocation boundary companies must plan, though no formal exclusivity rule surfaced.
interlinkages.source: multibagg.ai / oneindia EBP explainers; SATAT co-location coverage

interlinkages.detail: Product-line split: ECMS covers passive components (resistors, capacitors, connectors, relays, speakers), bare components (multi-layer PCBs), sub-assemblies (camera/display modules) and capital equipment — ~90% of mobile BoM; active components/semiconductors are explicitly excluded and routed to ISM. ECMS is described as a 'horizontal enabler working alongside ISM 2.0'. Incentives: turnover-linked, capex-linked and hybrid, with a portion of BOTH tied to employment generation; first-come-first-served allocation; 6-year tenure (1-year gestation).
interlinkages.source: drishtiias.com/daily-updates/daily-news-analysis/electronics-components-manufacturing-scheme; PIB PRID 2120240

interlinkages.detail: ECMS deliberately backfills the component layer beneath assembly-stage PLI — drishtiias frames the PLI criticism ('very little apart from assembly is done in India; component imports rose during peak export years') as the gap ECMS closes. Same company can hold assembly PLI and component ECMS approvals for different product lines.
interlinkages.source: drishtiias PLI + ECMS pages; investindia.gov.in team-india blog

interlinkages.detail: NOT VERIFIED THIS RUN: no fetched aggregator/explainer page delineated the ECMS vs MPMS product boundary; searches surfaced only ECMS-ISM boundary. Treat any ECMS/MPMS boundary claim in our dataset as unconfirmed pending a MeitY guideline check.
interlinkages.source: absence across drishtiias/vajiram/pwonlyias ECMS pages

interlinkages.detail: Timeline matters: AA/EOU/SEZ exports were originally excluded from RoDTEP; benefits were extended to them (separate lower rates in Appendix 4RE, vs Appendix 4R for normal exports) only until 5 Feb 2025, then lapsed, then RESTORED for exports from 1 Jun 2025 onwards (PIB PRID 2131526). So the current stance (as of mid-2026) is: AA holders CAN take RoDTEP on the same shipment, but at the segregated 4RE rates — the historical blanket exclusivity no longer holds.
interlinkages.source: pib.gov.in PRID 2131526; lakshmisri.com and leatherindia.org briefings; afleo.com RoDTEP guide

interlinkages.detail: Mutually exclusive by product chapter, not stackable on the same shipment: apparel/garments and made-ups (Ch. 61-63) get RoSCTL (separately notified rebate of state+central levies); all other eligible products get RoDTEP. One shipment line claims one of the two.
interlinkages.source: rodtepexchange.com FAQs; dhl.com RoDTEP guide; drishtiias RoDTEP page

interlinkages.detail: No exclusivity bar found: MeitY FAQ's blanket 'eligibility under PLI shall not affect eligibility under any other scheme' covers duty-remission/exemption instruments, and secondary comparative analyses (TaxTMI 'Comparative Analysis of MOOWR, PLI, SEZ, EOU, STPI, Advance Authorisation and EPCG') treat PLI (performance cash incentive) and EPCG (capital-goods duty exemption against export obligation) as distinct, combinable regimes. Caveat: sector-specific PLI guidelines can bar counting the same capex under multiple CENTRAL capex-subsidy schemes — duty exemption is not a capex subsidy, so the stack survives.
interlinkages.source: MeitY PLI FAQ Clause 3.6 (OFFICIAL) + taxtmi.com comparative analysis (SECONDARY)

interlinkages.detail: UNRESOLVED — flag for primary-source verification: the claim that SEZ units are historically excluded from PLI could NOT be confirmed or refuted from aggregators/explainers. MeitY's PLI FAQ contains zero SEZ references (grep of full extracted text). Circumstantial evidence cuts the other way: manufacturers run dual SEZ+DTA campuses (Flex Chennai) and 2025-26 SEZ-rule relaxations for electronics/semicon units suggest active accommodation. The correct primary check is each sector's PLI guideline definition of 'eligible sales' (domestic vs physical exports from SEZ).
interlinkages.source: MeitY FAQ (silent); taxtmi.com; angelone.in SEZ-relaxation news

interlinkages.detail: RDI (Rs 1 lakh cr, Cabinet 1 Jul 2025, launched at ESTIC 3 Nov 2025) never funds companies directly: a Special Purpose Fund under ANRF channels 50-year interest-free money to second-level fund managers (AIFs, DFIs, NBFCs) who lend long-tenor concessional/nil-interest loans or take equity in startups, and may contribute to Deep-Tech FoF. Governance: ANRF Governing Board (PM-chaired) → Executive Council → EGoS under Cabinet Secretary. It is a financing layer that stacks beneath any company also holding PLI/ECMS/SIGHT incentives.
interlinkages.source: pib.gov.in PRID 2141130; drishtiias RDI page; rdifund.anrf.gov.in

interlinkages.detail: The Rs 69,725 cr package (Cabinet ~Sep 2025) is itself a designed 4-pillar stack a shipyard can assemble: SBFAS (Rs 24,736 cr, extended to 31 Mar 2036) gives per-contract financial assistance; MDF (Rs 25,000 cr corpus) provides long-tenor capital; the Rs 5,000 cr Interest Incentivisation Fund cuts borrowing cost; SbDS (Rs 19,989 cr) funds capacity expansion incl. mega clusters — targeting 4.5 MGT annual capacity, ~Rs 4.5 lakh cr investment.
interlinkages.source: pib.gov.in PRID 2170573; maritimegateway.com; business-standard.com

interlinkages.detail: SIGHT (Rs 17,490 cr of NGHM's Rs 19,744 cr: Rs 4,440 cr electrolyser manufacturing Component-1; Rs 13,050 cr green H2/ammonia production Component-2) links to fertilizer demand via SECI aggregation: prices discovered for ~7.24 lakh MT/yr green ammonia supply to 13 fertilizer units, agreements signed for 6.7 lakh MTPA to 11 plants — making fertilizer plants the anchor offtake feeding SIGHT production incentives. 15 firms awarded 3,000 MW/yr electrolyser capacity.
interlinkages.source: mnre.gov.in NGHM; SECI SIGHT Component-II guidelines PDF; deccanherald

interlinkages.detail: Design-level complement from the Budget 2024-25 Rs 2 lakh cr employment package: ELI (Rs 99,446 cr, effective 1 Aug 2025-31 Jul 2027) pays on EPFO-verified headcount (Part A: one month EPF wage up to Rs 15,000 to first-timers in two instalments; Part B: up to Rs 3,000/month/additional-employee for 2 years to employers, salary ≤ Rs 1 lakh, +2 hires if <50 staff, +5 if ≥50, 6-month retention) while PLI pays on incremental sales — different bases, no exclusivity clause surfaced, so a manufacturer can hold both.
interlinkages.source: pib.gov.in PRID 2141127; epfindia.gov.in press release; drishtiias ELI page

interlinkages.detail: UCF (Rs 1 lakh cr central assistance FY26-FY31, extendable to FY34; Rs 10,000 cr in FY26) is structurally a leverage layer: Centre funds max 25% of project cost AND only if the city raises ≥50% from market sources (municipal bonds, loans, PPP) — i.e., it converges public grant with private capital rather than standing alone. Three verticals: Cities as Growth Hubs, Creative Redevelopment, Water & Sanitation. Eligible: cities ≥10 lakh population, all state/UT capitals, industrial cities ≥1 lakh.
interlinkages.source: pib.gov.in PRID 2098385 + 2227986; drishtiias Urban Challenge Fund page

interlinkages.detail: Internal category exclusivity: Category I Rs 4,050 cr reserved for PSUs (≤3 projects, lump sum Rs 1,350 cr or 15% capex, lower); Category II Rs 3,850 cr open to govt+private (Rs 1,000 cr or 15% capex per project); Category III Rs 600 cr demonstration/small-scale (Rs 100 cr or 15%). 8 projects worth Rs 6,233 cr under implementation. Part of the wider Rs 37,500 cr surface gasification outlay.
interlinkages.source: coal.gov.in guidelines PDF; pmindia.gov.in; business-standard

clarity_checks.explainer_account: drishtiias 'production-linked-incentive-scheme-1': launched March 2020 (3 sectors) expanding to 14; incentives on incremental sales over 5 years; Empowered Committee oversees; mobile exports $334M (FY18)→~$11B (FY23); criticisms: 'process of awarding incentives lacks clarity', no centralized outcome database, 'very little apart from assembly is done in India', component imports rose during peak export years
clarity_checks.note: Consistent with the Nov-2020 Rs 1.97 lakh cr / 14-sector cabinet framing (drishtiias omits the outlay figure entirely — a gap, not a contradiction). The assembly-vs-manufacture criticism and missing-database point are analytical additions worth carrying; the March-2020 date refers to the mobile/pharma/medical-devices pilot, Nov 2020 to the 10-sector expansion — both dates are correct, ensure our layer doesn't conflate them

clarity_checks.explainer_account: drishtiias: Budget 2026-27 raised ECMS outlay to Rs 40,000 cr; 6-year tenure, 1-year gestation for turnover incentives; covers ~90% of mobile BoM; semiconductors excluded to ISM 2.0; turnover/capex/hybrid incentives with employment-linked portions; FCFS allocation; projected Rs 10.34 lakh cr revenue, 1.41 lakh direct jobs. PIB: 29 more proposals approved (Rs 7,104 cr investment, Rs 84,515 cr production, 14,246 jobs)
clarity_checks.note: CAUTION: the widely-known original ECMS outlay at April-2025 notification was ~Rs 22,919 cr; drishtiias's Rs 40,000 cr is presented as a Budget 2026-27 enhancement. If our primary record still says 22,919, that's a genuine time-skew, not an error on either side — record both with dates

clarity_checks.explainer_account: agri.bot + official SOP: Rs 1 lakh cr, 3% interest subvention on loans up to Rs 2 cr (max 7 yrs), formal convergence with PMFME/PMKSY via portal using already-approved DPR + sanction letter
clarity_checks.note: Explainer and official SOP fully agree; the convergence module (Sep 2022, PIB 1861268) is itself the primary source

clarity_checks.explainer_account: Rs 19,744 cr total = Rs 17,490 cr SIGHT + Rs 1,466 cr pilots + Rs 400 cr R&D + Rs 388 cr other; SIGHT split Rs 4,440 cr electrolysers / Rs 13,050 cr production; 15 firms 3,000 MW electrolyser awards; 7.24 lakh MTPA ammonia price discovery for 13 fertilizer units; targets 5 MMT/yr, 125 GW RE, Rs 8 lakh cr investment, 6 lakh jobs
clarity_checks.note: Numbers match the Jan 2023 mission document exactly across MNRE, SECI and explainer accounts

clarity_checks.explainer_account: drishtiias + PIB: Rs 99,446 cr, Cabinet 1 Jul 2025, in force 1 Aug 2025-31 Jul 2027, >3.5 cr jobs target; Part A 1.92 cr first-timers, one month EPF wage ≤Rs 15,000 in 2 instalments (6 and 12 months, second tied to financial-literacy course); Part B employer Rs 3,000/mo/employee for 2 yrs, ~2.6 cr additional jobs
clarity_checks.note: Explainers (cleartax, pmfias, drishtiias) track the PIB release verbatim; note some explainers round to 'Rs 1 lakh crore' — use 99,446

clarity_checks.explainer_account: Rs 1 lakh cr over 6 years (Rs 20,000 cr FY26); Cabinet 1 Jul 2025, PM launch at ESTIC 3 Nov 2025; two-tier SPF-under-ANRF → 2nd-level fund managers (AIF/DFI/NBFC); concessional/nil-interest long loans + startup equity + possible Deep-Tech FoF contribution; EGoS under Cabinet Secretary; sunrise sectors: energy transition, quantum, robotics, AI, biotech, space
clarity_checks.note: drishtiias account matches PIB PRID 2141130 and rdifund.anrf.gov.in; the 'does not directly invest in industries' design point is the detail most often mangled elsewhere — drishtiias gets it right

clarity_checks.explainer_account: Rs 69,725 cr, 4 pillars: SBFAS Rs 24,736 cr extended to 31 Mar 2036; MDF Rs 25,000 cr + Rs 5,000 cr Interest Incentivisation Fund; SbDS Rs 19,989 cr for 4.5 MGT capacity; ~Rs 4.5 lakh cr investment, ~30 lakh jobs expected
clarity_checks.note: Secondary (maritimegateway, business-standard) and PIB PRID 2170573 agree; pillar sub-totals sum correctly (24,736+25,000+5,000+19,989 ≈ 74,725 — note the components as reported sum ABOVE 69,725; likely the 5,000 cr IIF sits inside MDF corpus accounting — flag for reconciliation)

clarity_checks.explainer_account: drishtiias/PIB: Rs 1 lakh cr central assistance FY2025-26 to 2030-31 (extendable FY34), Rs 10,000 cr FY26 allocation; Centre 25% of project cost, city must raise ≥50% from market; verticals: growth hubs, creative redevelopment, water/sanitation
clarity_checks.note: Budget-speech announcement (Feb 2025) and later Cabinet approval (PIB 2227986) are consistent; UCF is company-facing only indirectly (PPP concessionaires, municipal bond arrangers)

clarity_checks.explainer_account: drishtiias/coal.gov.in: Rs 8,500 cr in 3 categories (4,050 PSU / 3,850 mixed / 600 demo) with 15%-of-capex-capped lump sums; 8 projects Rs 6,233 cr under implementation
clarity_checks.note: Devdiscourse-style explainers describe 'up to 20% of Plant & Machinery cost in 4 milestone-linked instalments' while the coal.gov.in guideline framing is '15% of capex or fixed lump sum, whichever lower'. One of these is wrong or describes a different window — verify against the Feb 2024 guidelines PDF before our layer states a percentage

clarity_checks.explainer_account: drishtiias: 36 schemes/11 departments converged, Rs 24,000 cr/yr 2025-31 (Rs 1.44 lakh cr), 100 districts, 1.7 cr farmers, 117 KPIs, DDKY Samiti under Collector, 40% subsidies/30% infra/20% loans/10% training split
clarity_checks.note: Matches pmindia.gov.in cabinet note; the 40/30/20/10 split appears only in some explainers — treat as SECONDARY until found in an official annexure

clarity_checks.explainer_account: No scheme by this acronym found on any aggregator or explainer
clarity_checks.note: RESOLVED 2026-07-20: NIPU-2026 is REAL and the acronym is OFFICIAL -- PIB Cabinet release PRID 2284800 (15-Jul-2026) is titled 'Cabinet approves National Investment Policy for Urea-2026 for Atmanirbhar Bharat (NIPU-2026)': 8-9 gas-based urea plants, RoE band 12-16%. IAS aggregators (incl. Drishti, which suggested it was a truncated 'NIPUN') are simply ~5 days behind the primary source. A demonstration of the register's value: never drop an entry on aggregator silence alone.

aggregator_assessment.verdict: NOT useful for company-facing interlinkage work
aggregator_assessment.detail: Search/category pages (including 'Business & Entrepreneurship') are client-side React shells — zero scheme data in served HTML; the backing api.myscheme.gov.in returns 401 Unauthorized without an x-api-key; even a direct scheme-detail URL (schemes/dot-pli-scheme) served an error shell to a non-JS fetcher. Platform's own framing is citizen-eligibility discovery ('based upon the eligibility of the citizen', demographic-driven matching). It does index some company-facing schemes (a DoT PLI page exists per search engines) but carries no stacking/exclusivity metadata at all.

Static HTML is portal chrome (accessibility widgets, calendar, FAQ toggles); the scheme listing itself is JS-loaded — 0 scheme anchors extractable server-side. Even when rendered it is a link directory to nodal ministries, with no cross-scheme relationships.

aggregator_assessment.verdict: Citizen/rural focus; content unreachable to plain fetchers
aggregator_assessment.detail: Home page server-renders marketing copy; content routes (viewcontent/...) return ~11KB JS shells with no article text via curl. Historically decent scheme write-ups, but orientation is 'poor and vulnerable groups', not corporate incentives; no interlinkage layer.

Kept surfacing in searches as the canonical page for company-facing schemes (Ethanol Policy, National Green Hydrogen Mission scheme pages). For this project NSWS, not myscheme, is the official aggregator worth systematic scraping — not fetched this run, flagging as follow-up.

aggregator_assessment.site: drishtiias.com (and peers: vajiramandravi, pwonlyias, insightsonindia, forumias)
aggregator_assessment.verdict: Best per-scheme clarity, zero systematic interlinkage
aggregator_assessment.detail: UPSC explainers are current (already reflect Budget 2026-27 changes), numerically careful, and WebFetch-accessible without blocks. But every scheme is a standalone article; convergence/stacking appears only when it IS the news (PMDDKY, AIF convergence module). Nobody maintains a stacking matrix.

aggregator_assessment.site: Consultancy/subsidy-advisory sites (finraja, rmpsco, bizastra, afleo, taxtmi, agri.bot)
aggregator_assessment.detail: The center-state stacking knowledge (PLI+PSI SGST refunds, UP ECMS top-ups, Gujarat auto-sunrise, RoDTEP Appendix 4R vs 4RE mechanics, MOOWR/PLI/SEZ/EPCG comparisons) lives on commercial subsidy-consultant sites — commercially motivated but operationally specific. Treat as SECONDARY with per-claim verification against scheme guidelines; they are, however, the closest thing to a systematic interlinkage source that exists.

aggregator_assessment.conclusion: No aggregator — official or private — maintains interlinkage/stacking information systematically. The interlinkage layer being built here has no existing substitute; primary anchors for each edge are scheme-specific guideline PDFs (MeitY FAQ Clause 3.6, PMFME-AIF SOP, DGFT Appendix 4R/4RE, coal.gov.in guidelines) plus PIB releases.

site_access_issues.issue: Category/search pages are JS shells (curl returns app skeleton, 65-134KB, no scheme data; no usable __NEXT_DATA__ payload); api.myscheme.gov.in/search/v4/schemes returns {"message":"Unauthorized"} without an API key; scheme detail page served 'Something went wrong' error state to WebFetch
226KB static HTML is all portal chrome; scheme list is client-side loaded — no scheme links extractable
Home page fine (316KB, server-rendered); content pages (viewcontent/*) return ~11KB JS shells with no article text
Not directly fetched — search engines consistently ranked drishtiias/vajiram/pwonlyias/insightsonindia above it for every target scheme, so budget was spent there instead
No blocks; WebFetch rendered both target pages cleanly
Fetched OK (183KB); text extracted via pdftotext; contains the load-bearing Clause 3.6 stacking answer; contains ZERO references to 'SEZ' — SEZ-PLI stance remains unverified
~9 curl/WebFetch fetches + 14 web searches used; myscheme API and vikaspedia content are the two honest dead-ends; NSWS deliberately left unfetched as follow-up

verification_2026_07_20.method: Two agents: drishtiias.com (~19 fetches) + vajiram/pwonlyias/insights/clearias/forumias/nextias/visionias (~22 fetches). All SECONDARY. Verdicts per edge below.

tally.confirmed: 12 edges confirmed by at least one source family
tally.silent: 4 edges (incl. the two whose only anchor is the OFFICIAL MeitY FAQ)
tally.disputed: edge 23 coal-gasification percentage -- secondary sources contradict each other; official PDF is sole arbiter
tally.unresolved: edge 17 SEZ+PLI (with new Apr-2026 SEZ Rule 18 context)

1.note: No IAS site addresses same-capex stacking; MeitY FAQ 3.6 remains the sole (OFFICIAL) anchor

2.note: Drishti: UP ECMP-2025 'state-level incentives in addition to central benefits under ECMS' (magnitude not stated)

3.note: Drishti MoFPI Convergence Portal + ForumIAS: 3% subvention 'over and above the 35% subsidy'

5.note: Implicit: AHIDF eligible-activity list has no fisheries; boundary never stated explicitly

6.note: Direct contrast on both Drishti and ForumIAS/VisionIAS

7.note: 'Digital backbone linking existing schemes', no own corpus

8.note: Rs 24,000cr/yr = convergence envelope of constituent budgets; NOT a contradiction of no-separate-budget

9.note: 16.2bn litres capacity by Sep-2024, ~20bn by Nov-2025; mills earned Rs 1.29L cr from ethanol FY15-FY25

10.note: Pressmud (3-4% of cane) confirmed as CBG feedstock; physical co-location not discussed

11.note: 'Active components fall under ISM; ECMS focuses on passive components' (Insights); ~90% of mobile BoM (Drishti)

12.note: ECMS 90%-of-BoM + MPMS 1.5% sourcing bonus together document the architecture

13.note: Boundary resolved: MPMS = finished mobile phones (sales-linked 2.25-5%); ECMS = components/sub-assemblies/capital equipment; the MPMS +1.5% domestic-sourcing bonus is the coupling, not an overlap

14.note: Halted Feb-2025, reinstated from Jun-2025 (both); Appendix 4RE label not used by IAS sites; RoDTEP extended to Mar-2026

15.note: Drishti indirect: RoSCTL garments/made-ups-only + no-double-remission principle yields the exclusivity

16.note: No site discusses EPCG+PLI combination; nothing contradicts

17.note: STILL UNRESOLVED. New context: SEZ Rule 18 amended Apr-2026 (DTA sales for semiconductor/component SEZ units on duty payment + one-time concessional window Apr-26 to Mar-27) -- softens the boundary but does not resolve PLI eligibility. Primary check still needed

18.note: Two-tier SPF-under-ANRF -> AIFs/DFIs/NBFCs/FROs confirmed by both; guidelines+SPF approved Oct-2025; no disbursal reports as of Jul-2026

19.note: ARITHMETIC RESOLVED (two independent secondary corroborations): IIF Rs 5,000cr sits INSIDE MDF Rs 25,000cr (= MIF Rs 20,000cr equity 49% govt + IIF Rs 5,000cr grant/interest-subvention). 24,736 + 25,000 + 19,989 = 69,725 exactly; our 74,725 double-counted IIF

20.note: SECI 10-year contracts, 7.24L t/yr to 13 fertilizer plants, ~USD 2.5bn forex savings

21.note: Bases confirmed structurally distinct (EPFO headcount vs incremental sales); exclusivity never mentioned anywhere -- 'no exclusivity' unopposed but unaffirmed

22.note: 25% centre + minimum 50% market sources verbatim; ~Rs 4L cr catalysis target

23.note: SECONDARY SOURCES DISAGREE AMONG THEMSELVES: Drishti ties '20% of P&M in 4 milestone instalments' to the Rs 8,500cr scheme; PWOnlyIAS ties '15% of capex or lump sum whichever LOWER, 2 instalments, category caps 1,350/1,000/100cr' to the same Feb-2024 guidelines; VisionIAS-family ties 20%/4 to the separate Rs 37,500cr surface-gasification scheme. KEEP FLAGGED: the coal.gov.in guideline PDF is the only arbiter -- do not cite a percentage until read from it

new_edges_2026_07_20.detail: EXPLICIT in scheme terms (quoted by VisionIAS): E-DRIVE incentives 'are independent of and in addition to incentives given under PLI-Auto and PLI-ACC' -- demand-side + supply-side stack; also complements GST cuts and s.80EEB
MPMS pays +1.5% on eligible sales for domestic sourcing of components/sub-assemblies -- designed demand-pull for the ECMS-incentivised layer (+3% for Indian-brand design/R&D)
ECMS framed as horizontal enabler across the full electronics stack: ISM=active components, ECMS=passives/sub-assemblies/equipment, MPMS=finished devices, EMC=clusters
AIF loans carry CGTMSE guarantee cover ALONGSIDE the 3% subvention -- two stacked benefits in one loan
AIF has a built-in GENERAL convergence clause with any other scheme -- wider than the PMFME/PMKSY edges
